license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.beqpjdvn
title: Cryogrinding protocol : mecanic lysis of planktonic filter for RNA/DNA extraction
author: Sarah Romac

steps_raw.instruction: Finish the assembly of the cryogrinding tube : insert the magnet inside the tube and close the tube with the other extremity containing the hole.
Put the tube in the FreezerMill chamber, and check the liquid nitrogen level and close the FreezerMill.
Set up the FreezerMill cryogrinding conditions : Precool: 00:02:00 Run time: 00:01:00 Cycles number: 1 Rate: 5 knocks per sec . (respect roughly this condition!).

Replace the Falcon50 in the liquid nitrogen in the Becher and process to the cryogrinding of the second part of the 142mm filter. For that, take the Falcon50 containing the half-part left of the 142mm filter and repeat steps 9 to 18 .

Disassemble the cryogrinding tubes. Wash thoroughly all the parts of the cryogrinding tubes, and the FreezerMill extractor using a teethbrush to remove powder residues following this order : - 1% diluted bleach ; - tap water ; - milliQ water ; - EtOH 70% . Decontaminate the bench, and Becher using RNase away for next sample cryogrinding. Put all the small equipment cited above (including the teethbrush) on an alumin paper and incubate them into UV-light at least 20min. (in a PCR hood or a cross-linker for instance, https://www.dutscher.com/frontoffice/product?produitId=0O-78-39 )
Check the level using a 10mL pipette (maximum level of liquid ntrogen should correspond to 5 mL on pipette's graduation).

Pour a half the 1 L Becher with liquid nitrogen. Dive the sample tube containing the 142mm filter in the Becher to keep it deep-frozeen. Always check the volume of liquid nitrogen inside the Becher to avoid degradation of sample.
Close delicately the lid of the FreezerMill.
Fill the Freezer Mill tank : pour Liquid Ntrogen in the tank until the "up limit" graduation. Always check the volume of liquid nitrogen inside the FreezerMill.
Wait for 20-30 min until the Freezer Mill reachs in temperature.
Assemble the bases for 2 FreezerMill cryogrinding tubes :
Put the 2 half-assembled tubes in the Becher .

Take the sample tube with the tweezers, open the tube and take the filter with the tweezers. If the filter resists, delicately break the tube using the crab tweezer keeping holding the tube with the tweezer.

Take away the filter with the tweezer and cut the half of the filter using the cizors and put it in a Falcon50. Put this Falcon in the liquid Nitrogen. Cut the other half-part filter again in twice and put these 2 pieces in one of the 2 frozen cryogrinding tube.
Start the run.
Put a sterile Falcon50 correctly annotated (Sample code) in the Becher containing liquid Nitrogen for cooling. This Falcon will be used to collect the final powder from the cryogrinded filter.

When the run is "complete", open delicately the FreezerMill and take the cryogrinding tube using the FreezerMill extractor. Avoid to touch the cryoginding tube with the cryogenic gloves. Preferably use some Kimwipe papers.
Put the cryogrinding tube in the Becher containing liquid nitrogen.
Open delicately the cryogrinding tube using the FreezerMill extractor, and remove the magnet using tweezers.

Transfer the powder obtained in the Falcon50 tube by directly inversing the the cryogrinding tube inside the Falcon50. Afterthat, use the spatula to collect a maximum of powder. Recover most of material you can using the spatula.
At the end of the 2 cryogrinding runs, store the Falcon50 tube containing the powder at -80°C until RNA/DNA extraction process.
Choose the format of the filter for next steps :

Pour a half the 1 L Becher with liquid nitrogen. Dive the sample tube containing the 142mm filter in the Becher to keep it deep-frozeen. Always check the volume of liquid nitrogen inside the Becher to avoid degradation of sample.
Assemble the bases for 1 FreezerMill cryogrinding tube :
Put the half-assembled tube in the Becher .

Take the sample tube with the tweezers, open the tube and take the filter with the tweezers. If the filter resists, delicately break the tube using the crab tweezer keeping holding the tube with the tweezer.
Take away the filter with the tweezer and put it in the frozen cryogrinding tube.
Finish the assembly of the cryogrinding tube : insert the magnet inside the tube and close the tube with the other extremity containing the hole.
Put the tube in the FreezerMill chamber, and check the liquid nitrogen level and close the FreezerMill.
Put a sterile Falcon50 correctly annotated (Sample code) in the Becher containing liquid Nitrogen for cooling. This Falcon will be used to collect the final powder from the cryogrinded filter.
Set up the FreezerMill cryogrinding conditions : Precool: 00:02:00 Run time: 00:01:00 Cycles number: 1 Rate: 5 knocks per sec . (respect roughly this condition!).
Start the run.

When the run is "complete", open delicately the FreezerMill and take the cryogrinding tube using the FreezerMill extractor. Avoid to touch the cryoginding tube with the cryogenic gloves. Preferably use some Kimwipe papers.
Put the cryogrinding tube in the Becher containing liquid nitrogen.
Open delicately the cryogrinding tube using the FreezerMill extractor, and remove the magnet using tweezers.

Transfer the powder obtained in the Falcon50 tube by directly inversing the the cryogrinding tube inside the Falcon50. Afterthat, use the spatula to collect a maximum of powder. Recover most of material you can using the spatula.
Store the Falcon50 tube containing the powder at -80°C until RNA/DNA extraction process.

Disassemble the cryogrinding tubes. Wash thoroughly all the parts of the cryogrinding tubes, and the FreezerMill extractor using a teethbrush to remove powder residues following this order : - 1% diluted bleach ; - tap water ; - milliQ water ; - EtOH 70% . Decontaminate the bench, and Becher using RNase away for next sample cryogrinding. Put all the small equipment cited above (including the teethbrush) on an alumin paper and incubate them into UV-light at least 20min. (in a PCR hood or a cross-linker for instance, https://www.dutscher.com/frontoffice/product?produitId=0O-78-39 )
